name: Provenance Chains
overview: Every entity in TrustDB carries a ProvenanceChain that tracks who created it, what source it came from, when it was created/modified, and the full chain of transformations. ProvenanceChains are stored inline with LeafEntry data (no joins required), and the Temporal Co-occurrence Index (TCI) auto-records entity co-occurrence patterns for relationship discovery.

howItWorks.title: Inline Storage
howItWorks.description: ProvenanceChain is embedded directly in the LeafEntry alongside the value and ConfidenceEnvelope. This means reading an entity's provenance requires zero additional lookups — it arrives with the data itself.

howItWorks.title: Chain Extension
howItWorks.description: Each mutation appends a ProvenanceEntry to the chain, recording the actor (user/agent/system), timestamp, operation type, and source reference. The chain is append-only and immutable once written.

howItWorks.title: TCI Auto-Recording
howItWorks.description: The Temporal Co-occurrence Index monitors write patterns and automatically records when entities are frequently written together within temporal windows. This builds implicit relationship graphs without explicit edge creation.

howItWorks.title: Zero-Overhead Query
howItWorks.description: Since provenance is inline with data, querying 'who created this entity and what was its source?' adds zero I/O overhead. The provenance arrives in the same read that fetches the value.

comparisons.source: Separate provenance tables with foreign keys

comparisons.narrative: Provenance in traditional systems requires separate tables, graphs, or services that must be joined at query time. TrustDB's inline approach eliminates this join tax entirely. The automatic TCI layer goes further by discovering entity relationships that were never explicitly declared.

competitors.approach: Separate provenance tables conforming to W3C PROV-DM
competitors.limitation: Requires joins to reconstruct provenance; scales poorly with chain length

competitors.approach: Provenance as graph nodes with CREATED_BY/DERIVED_FROM edges
competitors.limitation: Adds graph traversal cost; provenance nodes compete with data nodes for memory
Separate service with network overhead; eventual consistency with data store
Pipeline-level lineage only; no per-entity provenance at storage level
